title: CSS Units
content:
### CSS Units

CSS has several units for specifying sizes, spacing, and lengths.

---

### Absolute Units (Fixed Size)

| Unit | Name | Equivalent |
|------|------|------------|
| `px` | Pixels | 1px = 1/96th inch |
| `pt` | Points | 1pt = 1/72nd inch |
| `cm` | Centimeters | |
| `mm` | Millimeters | |
| `in` | Inches | 1in = 96px |
| `pc` | Picas | 1pc = 12pt |

**Use `px` for screen. Avoid `pt`, `cm`, `in` for web.**

---

### Relative Units (Recommended)

| Unit | Relative To |
|------|-------------|
| `em` | Font size of parent element |
| `rem` | Font size of root element (`html`) |
| `%` | Parent element's size |
| `vw` | 1% of viewport width |
| `vh` | 1% of viewport height |
| `vmin` | 1% of smaller viewport dimension |
| `vmax` | 1% of larger viewport dimension |
| `ch` | Width of character "0" |
| `ex` | Height of character "x" |

---

### em vs rem

```css
html { font-size: 16px; }

.parent {
  font-size: 2em;   /* 32px (2 * 16px) */
}

.child-em {
  font-size: 2em;   /* 64px (2 * 32px from parent) */
}

.child-rem {
  font-size: 2rem;  /* 32px (2 * 16px from root) */
}
```

---

### Viewport Units

```css
.fullscreen {
  width: 100vw;    /* Full viewport width */
  height: 100vh;   /* Full viewport height */
}

.half {
  width: 50vw;     /* Half the viewport */
}
```

---

> **Best Practice:** Use `rem` for font sizes, `%` for widths, `px` for borders/shadows, `em` for padding/margin relative to font size.
